---
title: "MP122: Dual-Track Subrepo Architecture Principle"
subtitle: "Separation of framework and application layers through independent subrepo management"
chapter: "CH00"
category: "meta-principle"
number: "MP122"
date-created: "2025-09-30"
date-modified: "2025-09-30"
author: "Claude (Principle Revisor)"
type: "meta-principle"
law: "System Architecture Law"
article:
  number: 122
  title: "Dual-Track Subrepo Architecture"
related_to:
  - "MP093: Script Separation Principle"
  - "MP044: Functor-Module Correspondence"
  - "MP097: Principle-Implementation Separation"
  - "MP104: Script Organization Evolution"
  - "SO_R001: Directory Structure"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article 122: Dual-Track Subrepo Architecture {#overview}

**System Architecture Law Article 122 (Dual-Track Subrepo Architecture)**

The system SHALL adopt a dual-track subrepo architecture that separates the **framework layer** (`global_scripts`) from the **application layer** (`update_scripts`) as independent git subrepos, achieving an optimal balance between stability and flexibility while maximizing cross-project reusability.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Architecture Principle
- **global_scripts/**: Framework layer - stable, reusable, cross-project components
- **update_scripts/**: Application layer - business-specific, frequently updated logic
- Both managed as **independent git subrepos** with separate version control
- **Unidirectional dependency**: update_scripts → global_scripts (never reverse)
:::

# Section 1: Architectural Philosophy {#philosophy}

## Subsection 1: Design Rationale {#rationale}

The dual-track architecture recognizes that software systems have two fundamentally different types of code:

1. **Framework Code**: General-purpose, stable, reusable across projects
2. **Application Code**: Business-specific, frequently changing, project-specific

By separating these into independent subrepos, we achieve:
- **Independent evolution** of framework and application layers
- **Cross-project sharing** of framework components
- **Isolated change management** for different change velocities
- **Clear architectural boundaries** enforced by git

## Subsection 2: Category Theory Foundation {#category-theory}

From a category theory perspective:

- **Objects**: The two subrepos represent distinct categories
- **Morphisms**: Dependencies flow unidirectionally (application → framework)
- **Functors**: Interfaces between layers preserve categorical structure
- **Natural Transformations**: Version upgrades maintain compatibility

# Section 2: Framework Layer (global_scripts) {#framework-layer}

## Subsection 1: Characteristics {#framework-characteristics}

The `global_scripts` subrepo contains:

**Content Types**:
- Reusable utility functions (`04_utils/`)
- Database abstraction layers (`01_db/`, `02_db_utils/`)
- Configuration frameworks (`03_config/`)
- UI component libraries (`10_rshinyapp_components/`)
- System principles and documentation (`00_principles/`)
- Language frameworks (NSQL, AIMETA)
- Testing utilities (`98_test/`)

**Properties**:
- **Stability**: Changes infrequently, maintains backward compatibility
- **Generality**: Solves general problems, not specific business cases
- **Reusability**: Designed for use across multiple projects
- **Versioning**: Semantic versioning with clear deprecation policies

## Subsection 2: Management Rules {#framework-management}

```yaml
# Subrepo configuration for global_scripts
remote: git@github.com:org/global_scripts.git
branch: main
version_policy: semantic
breaking_changes: major_version_only
deprecation_period: 3_months
cross_project: true
```

# Section 3: Application Layer (update_scripts) {#application-layer}

## Subsection 1: Characteristics {#application-characteristics}

The `update_scripts` subrepo contains:

**Content Types**:
- ETL pipelines (`*_ETL*.R` scripts)
- Data derivation logic (`*_D*.R` scripts)
- Business-specific transformations
- Platform-specific integrations
- Migration and update scripts
- One-time fixes and patches

**Properties**:
- **Volatility**: Changes frequently with business requirements
- **Specificity**: Solves specific business problems
- **Statefulness**: Modifies system state and data
- **Versioning**: Feature-based versioning aligned with business releases

## Subsection 2: Management Rules {#application-management}

```yaml
# Subrepo configuration for update_scripts
remote: git@github.com:org/project_update_scripts.git
branch: main
version_policy: feature_based
change_frequency: high
project_specific: true
depends_on: global_scripts
```

# Section 4: Dependency Architecture {#dependencies}

## Subsection 1: Permitted Dependencies {#permitted}

**update_scripts → global_scripts** ✅
```r
# In update_scripts/platform_ETL01.R
source("../global_scripts/01_db/dbConnect.R")
source("../global_scripts/04_utils/fn_process.R")
```

**Cross-project global_scripts sharing** ✅
```bash
# Project A and Project B can share the same global_scripts
ProjectA/scripts/global_scripts -> shared/global_scripts.git
ProjectB/scripts/global_scripts -> shared/global_scripts.git
```

## Subsection 2: Forbidden Dependencies {#forbidden}

**global_scripts → update_scripts** ❌
```r
# NEVER in global_scripts
source("../../update_scripts/specific_logic.R")  # FORBIDDEN
```

**Circular subrepo dependencies** ❌
```yaml
# FORBIDDEN configuration
global_scripts:
  depends_on: update_scripts  # Creates circular dependency
```

# Section 5: Implementation Patterns {#patterns}

## Subsection 1: Subrepo Initialization {#initialization}

```bash
# Initialize dual-track architecture
cd scripts/

# Add framework layer as subrepo
git subrepo clone git@github.com:org/global_scripts.git global_scripts

# Add application layer as subrepo
git subrepo clone git@github.com:org/update_scripts.git update_scripts

# Verify structure
git subrepo status
```

## Subsection 2: Update Patterns {#update-patterns}

**Framework Updates** (Low frequency, high impact):
```bash
# Pull latest framework changes
git subrepo pull scripts/global_scripts

# Run compatibility tests
Rscript scripts/global_scripts/98_test/test_compatibility.R

# Update only after verification
git commit -m "Update global_scripts to v2.1.0"
```

**Application Updates** (High frequency, low impact):
```bash
# Regular business logic updates
cd scripts/update_scripts
# Make changes...

# Push changes frequently
git subrepo push scripts/update_scripts
```

# Section 6: Decision Matrix {#decision-matrix}

## When to place code in each layer:

| Criteria | global_scripts | update_scripts |
|----------|----------------|----------------|
| **Reusability** | Cross-project | Project-specific |
| **Stability** | Stable API | Frequently changing |
| **State** | Stateless functions | Stateful operations |
| **Business Logic** | Generic patterns | Specific rules |
| **Dependencies** | Minimal, stable | Can depend on global |
| **Testing** | Comprehensive unit tests | Integration tests |
| **Documentation** | Full API docs | Usage examples |
| **Versioning** | Semantic | Feature-based |

# Section 7: Benefits {#benefits}

The dual-track subrepo architecture provides:

1. **Separation of Concerns**: Clear boundary between framework and application
2. **Independent Evolution**: Each layer evolves at its natural pace
3. **Cross-Project Reusability**: Framework improvements benefit all projects
4. **Risk Isolation**: Application changes don't affect framework stability
5. **Team Scalability**: Different teams can own different layers
6. **Version Control**: Independent git histories for different concerns
7. **Deployment Flexibility**: Can update layers independently
8. **Testing Strategy**: Different test approaches for each layer

# Section 8: Migration Strategy {#migration}

## For existing monolithic codebases:

### Phase 1: Identify {#identify}
```r
# Analyze codebase for separation candidates
source("scripts/global_scripts/00_principles/analyze_separation.R")
report <- analyze_code_separation(".")
```

### Phase 2: Extract {#extract}
```r
# Extract reusable components to global_scripts
move_to_global <- report$reusable_functions
migrate_functions(move_to_global, "scripts/global_scripts/04_utils/")
```

### Phase 3: Establish {#establish}
```bash
# Convert directories to subrepos
git subrepo init scripts/global_scripts
git subrepo init scripts/update_scripts

# Push to remote repositories
git subrepo push scripts/global_scripts
git subrepo push scripts/update_scripts
```

# Section 9: Relationship to Other Principles {#relationships}

## Supporting Principles:

**MP093 (Script Separation)**: Provides the conceptual separation this architecture implements physically

**MP044 (Functor-Module Correspondence)**: Organizes content within each track

**MP097 (Principle-Implementation Separation)**: Principles in global_scripts, implementations in update_scripts

**SO_R001 (Directory Structure)**: Defines the internal organization of each track

## Enhanced Principles:

This architecture enhances existing principles by:
- Providing git-enforced boundaries
- Enabling cross-project sharing
- Supporting independent versioning
- Facilitating team collaboration

# Section 10: Enforcement and Compliance {#enforcement}

## Code Review Checklist:

- [ ] New reusable functions go in global_scripts
- [ ] Business-specific logic stays in update_scripts
- [ ] No reverse dependencies (global → update)
- [ ] Proper subrepo commit messages
- [ ] Version compatibility verified before updates
- [ ] Documentation updated in both layers
- [ ] Tests appropriate to each layer

## CI/CD Integration:

```yaml
# .github/workflows/dual-track-ci.yml
name: Dual-Track CI
on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  framework-tests:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
      - name: Test global_scripts
        run: |
          cd scripts/global_scripts
          Rscript 98_test/run_all_tests.R

  application-tests:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: framework-tests
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
      - name: Test update_scripts
        run: |
          cd scripts/update_scripts
          Rscript test_integration.R
```

---

*Effective Date: 2025-09-30*
*System Architecture Law Article 122*
*Architectural Pattern: Dual-Track Subrepo*
*Status: Active*
